programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Woodworker Neil needs to focus to achieve his goals. Embroiderer Catherine dreams of a successful business but is spreading herself too thin. Can Dom and his mentors help?

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where knowledge equals speed. Justin, Dean, Serena, Joe and Andrea race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000.

programme.title: Pointless

programme.title: Beyond Paradise

programme.title: The Claudia Winkleman Show
programme.desc: Claudia looks back at some of the highlights from the series, featuring her studio audience and an array of star-studded sofa guests, including Lisa Kudrow, Jennifer Saunders, Niall Horan, Guz Khan, Jeff Goldblum, James McAvoy, Rita Wilson, Jamie Dornan, Anna Faris, Jimmy Carr, Ralph Fiennes, Rachel Zegler, Jamie Dornan and many more.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: In Prentisstown, Todd has been brought up to believe that the Spackle released a germ that killed all the women and unleashed Noise on the remaining men. After discovering a patch of silence out in the swamp, his surrogate parents immediately tell him that he has to run, leaving him with only a map of New World, a message, and many unanswered questions. He soon discovers the source of the silence: a girl, named Viola.

programme.title: Scams & Scandals
programme.desc: BBC News goes undercover to expose legal advisers charging migrants thousands of pounds to fabricate immigration claims. Some migrants are being encouraged to pretend to be gay to claim asylum. They are advised to gather untrue evidence including supporting letters and photos at LGBT events, for their applications. Others are encouraged to make false allegation of domestic abuse. The investigation finds advisers exploiting migrants and abusing safeguards designed to protect those truly at risk.

Also, Jenson Button is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car.

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Orbiting a quiet backwater planet, the massed forces of the universe's deadliest species gather, drawn to a mysterious message that echoes out to the stars. The Doctor must learn what this signal means for his own fate and that of the universe.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: It's Christmas at Button House and Mike and Alison are heading to Mike's parents to celebrate with family. As Mike piles up the Tupperware with what he insists are only side dishes, despite one clearly being an entire turkey, Kitty expresses her disappointment at not spending Christmas day with Alison. Alison promises that they'll be back by the evening and what's more, reveals to the ghosts that there is a Christmas surprise for each of them in the library.

programme.title: Ghosts
programme.desc: It's coming up to Christmas, and Mike's mum is staying. Betty quickly overstays her welcome, but Alison and Mike don't quite have the heart to tell her. Just as she decides to leave of her own volition, Betty suspects something is terribly wrong.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda isn't your average girly girl. At 6 ft 1, Miranda gets called Sir once too often, much to the amusement of her childhood friend Stevie who manages Miranda's joke shop. Miranda bumps into her old friend Gary, who is working in the restaurant next door as the new chef. When he asks her out for a drink she gets over excited by the prospect of her first real date and decides it's time to try to be more girly.

programme.title: Miranda
programme.desc: Miranda is rather British when it comes to sex, or shenanigans as she likes to call it. Trying to overcome her hang-ups she decides the way forward with Gary would be to create a romantic moment so he'll see her in a sexual light. Meanwhile, Stevie persuades her to join an evening class to learn French, the language of love.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Actor Richard returns home from London to visit his dad Ken, a year on from the passing of his mum Jean. But on arriving, he realises his dad may need more help than he'd originally thought.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard decides the time has come to clear Ken's house of all his junk or as Ken calls it, his memories. As Ken's suspicions over Richard's true motives grow, the arrival of Mr Oddjob and Richard's former school nemesis, complicates matters further.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard offers to take Ken to the local golf club, unaware that he's about to play a much bigger part in integrating his dad back into the community than he'd originally intended.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard battles medical receptionists in order to secure Ken a doctor's appointment and is asked to impart his acting wisdom to the local am-dram group.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard secures an audition and endeavours to track down Ken's oldest friend, who Ken lost touch with years ago, in order to invite him to Ken's surprise 75th birthday party.

programme.title: Only Child
programme.desc: Richard struggles with finding the right time to tell Ken his big news as the two of them spend the day touring Forres in search of the ideal spot to scatter Jean's ashes.

programme.title: Two Pints of Lager and a Packet of Crisps
programme.desc: Jonny is reeling after being chatted up by Louise's Dad. He decides to become more macho, but his plan backfires with shocking consequences. Louise is feeling let down by her Dad and becomes a really bad alcoholic. And Donna refuses to have Gaz's baby, leaving him only one option for getting the child he longs for.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Bristol, distillery manager Luke is relying on caffeine to keep stress at bay - but Clive Myrie has a gentler, smarter swap in mind. If Luke traded his coffee and energy drinks for tea, the benefits could be huge. Studies show that drinking black tea regularly can reduce anxiety, calm the brain and even support heart health. A simple brew with surprisingly powerful perks.

programme.title: Ronan Keating's Wild Atlantic
programme.desc: Kerry is known as The Kingdom in Ireland, and it's Ronan's next stop on his Wild Atlantic journey. He joins his big brother Gary on the stunning Lough Leane in Killarney for a fishing trip, one that they first went on together 40 years ago. He also meets a couple who make traditional handcrafted leather items and gets to work on a guitar strap, before running onto the pitch at Dr Crokes GAA club to play some hurling, the fastest field sport in the world.

programme.desc: Even for a giant seven-foot bear called Grizzly, it's not easy getting rid of a group of pesky lemmings, but it's all fun seeing them constantly try to outsmart each other with cheeky plans!

programme.title: Mush-Mush and the Mushables
programme.sub-title: Race The Beast; Surprise, It's Spring; The Missing Mushlet; The Hero Trap; Bird Call
programme.desc: Mush-Mush and his best friends Chep and Lilit have all kinds of adventures in the forest. They are the protectors of the forest, and they all have a unique power.

programme.title: Mush-Mush and the Mushables
programme.sub-title: A Frog Of A Problem; Fool The Forest; Mushroomates; Race The Beast
programme.desc: The Mushables try to prank the forest by pretending to be muddy. When ants invade Fung and Miff’s tree, our Mushlers offer for them to move in. Mush-Mush and his friends are determined to win the bark sledge race.

programme.title: Moley
programme.sub-title: Keeper for a Day; For All Molekind; Magic Rectangle; Three Gentlemen of Moletown; Guacamoley
programme.desc: Revolving around an adventurous mole's quest with friends to broker peace between humans and moles. Following the underground adventures of the charmingly optimistic young mole, Moley.

programme.title: Tom & Jerry
programme.sub-title: Jerry's Cousin; Johann Mouse; Just Ducky
programme.desc: Jerry asks his tough cousin Muscles Mouse to help him deal with Tom. Tom learns to play the piano to capture the waltzing Johann Mouse. Jerry tries to teach a newly-hatched duckling to swim.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.sub-title: Now You Sia, Now You Don't!
programme.desc: An evil demon ghost takes the shape of singer-songwriter Sia and steals countless gems and jewellery. The gang tries to catch Sia's ghostly doppelganger and prove her innocence.

programme.title: Tom and Jerry in New York
programme.sub-title: Planet of Mice; Ball of Fun; Big Apple; Flamingo A-Go-Go
programme.desc: Tom has a nightmare about a super-intelligent mouse takeover; Jerry inherits a hamster ball; an epic chase through NYC turns into a musical tribute to the city; at the zoo, a flock of flamingoes protects Jerry and Tuffy from Tom.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean makes it to the top of the ladder and gets stuck. Mr Bean bites off more than he can chew when he attempts DIY dentistry.

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.sub-title: Dam Busters; Dandy Do-Gooders; Day of the Jackalope
programme.desc: Life is a never-ending cat-and-mouse game for Tom and Jerry, with their shenanigans spawning chaos, laughter and occasional moments of collaboration.

programme.title: Grizzy and the Lemmings
programme.sub-title: Bear Gliding; The Sound of a Lemming; Bear in the Wind
programme.desc: The Lemmings play kite surf with a homemade kite. Grizzy snatches the last jar of chocolate spread from the Lemmings. In exchange, he offers them the toy stuck inside the lid, a mini-flute. Lemmings use the fan to float like flags.

programme.title: Scooby-Doo! and Krypto, Too!
programme.desc: Scooby and the gang join forces with Superman's dog when members of the Justice League mysteriously disappear.

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Papa tells his fractured version of the Jack and the Beanstalk story to the Bavarian mouse trio.

programme.title: Tom and Jerry in New York
programme.sub-title: Too Much Monkey Business; Doggie Championship; Snow Day; Toots the Terrible; The Spa's the Limit; The Hairy Dignitary; Year of the Mouse; Relativity
programme.desc: Tom attempts to keep a VIP chimpanzee from the hotel's bananas. Spike injures himself while training for a contest. Toots wants to capture Jerry in order to spend more time with Tom.

programme.title: Tom and Jerry
programme.sub-title: The Million Dollar Cat; The Milky Waif; The Mansion Cat; The Invisible Mouse; The Haunted Mouse; The Guided Mouse-ille
programme.desc: Tom and Jerry, a cat and mouse, attempt to outwit one another as they weave through Singapore, exploring the city's modern skyscrapers, colourful neighbourhoods and luscious city-in-nature experiences.

programme.sub-title: Bean at the Museum; Stick It

programme.title: Tom & Jerry

programme.desc: A penguin decides to help Grizzy catch piles of fish, and the pair strike up a friendship. The Lemmings perform acrobatic high jumps, competing for the winning prize. The Lemmings come up with a ride worthy of a county fair in the living room.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: Mr Bean tries to take a photo of a rare bird, but some squirrels get in the way! Mr Bean thinks Scrapper has swallowed a vital part of his remote-controlled boat.

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series

programme.desc: It's a hot day in Playtime Towers, so Bella helps Lift cool down with a paper fan. Together, they visit Hettie in the Ice Disco, who has a flapping paper fan dance!

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing finds a frog in the garden and wants to keep it, so he and Sula make the frog a house in a bucket. But Froggy doesn't like her new home and hops out. Maybe they've not thought enough about what Froggy really needs in her home.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `cucumber', and goes on a whirlwind word adventure. See cucumbers that are crunchy, mushy in the middle and curly. Dee meets a cool cucumber, and a very cute baby one. The journey is repeated with some special surprises along the way, and everyone has a boogie when Dee's friend finally succeeds in saying today's word.

programme.title: Lu & the Bally Bunch
programme.desc: Declan causes a stir when he decides not to say please after Lu sets up shop to sell a few toys, even though all the bugs had been courteous enough to say please when taking their items.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Dana aims to cheer up Wilf and asks the Piggies to find something to make him happy. However, Wilf exploits their kindness by requesting a honking vehicle.

programme.title: Twirlywoos
programme.desc: The Twirlywoos are in a kitchen, where they watch a man turn on a tap and fill a glass with water. When he leaves the Twirlywoos come out and turn the tap on. They fill a glass with water and then start to find other things around the kitchen to fill up.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends bake some delicious pancakes, whilst Mr Tumble and Baker Tumble make some of their own to feed to the other hungry Tumbles.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Laa-Laa and Po drive the Tubby Car to the Hidey Hup and take turns on the Tubby Bouncy. Tinky Winky and Dipsy arrive and they have a go at bouncing too.

programme.title: The Baby Club
programme.desc: Giovanna and Baby Bear welcome babies Remi, George, Zadie, Idris, Felicity, Tommy and their grown-ups to The Baby Club. Inside everyone's bags in this episode are cardboard boxes which are explored through discovery, play, song and story. Grown-ups at home are encouraged to take part by hiding baby appropriate cardboard boxes inside a fabric bag or pillowcase for them to reveal and explore with their baby! The fun song `The Wheels on the Bus!' features, as does a story involving Baby Bear.

programme.title: I Spy, You Spy
programme.desc: Elsee visits a building site with her new friend Dreya to play a game of I Spy.

programme.title: What's in Your Plant Pot?
programme.desc: Gardening expert Sue introduces the Fact Pack to roses. They discover there are hundreds of types, why roses can smell lovely, and how their smell and flavour is in many different things.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: It's a very special day in the attic, but Rebecca seems to have forgotten all about it. Murphy has an inspirational idea for a 'bee-autiful' yogurt pot bee, the Mini Monsters transform paper plates into snappy-mouthed animals, and Rebecca gets a call from her uncle at the zoo. Finally, the Recycling Express inspires Murphy to help Rebecca turn different-sized cardboard boxes into a cake.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: The gang rescue a trapped baby Watermelonsuchus with the help of some Lycheemodus.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: While camping in the bush, Bluey makes a new friend who doesn't speak the same language.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: As Roly makes his usual noise in the Clubhouse, Duggee and the Squirrels reminisce about how completely different Roly was when he first arrived. Hiding behind his dad, Roly wasn't loud at all but impossibly quiet. As Duggee shows Roly around the Clubhouse, he squeals, hides and hangs off his dad, and just seems altogether too shy to fit in. Duggee has a great idea to introduce Roly to Norrie, thinking a friend will help him to settle in.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Mrs Weaver returns with a song all about seasons. It's a celebration of spring, summer, autumn and winter.

programme.title: Let's Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to the park to feed the birds at the pond. On the way, they race like racing cars, spot the different coloured flowers, listen to the thunder and even meet a Stick Man tree!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Meet sisters Kyra and Khloe as they go on a big family day out to a farm. There are lots of exciting things to do, including a pony ride. They explore an amazing maze where the girls find a giant sunflower.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Kay has invited his friends to taste his Polish dill pickle soup, which his mum has shown him how to cook all by himself. Dill pickle is a popular Polish ingredient and Kay gets his friends to taste it before they eat the soup. Not all of his friends like the taste at first, but Kay hopes they will all enjoy the finished soup. As well as dill pickle, he uses spring onions, potatoes and carrot in his soup.

programme.title: Maddie's Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is learning about helpful vehicles, and so she gets on board a training rescue mission with a lifeboat team to find out how their equipment works.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Tinks helps Charli with some litter picking in the park. But the gate to the bins is locked, and the recycling van is on its way. Can Tinks fetch park ranger Pauline in time?

programme.title: Steve and Aneeshwar Go Wild
programme.desc: Steve and Aneeshwar meet some of the zoo's acrobats, a family of fossa who are incredible climbers and enjoy jumping high up in the trees. Next, they go to see the leaf-tailed gecko, which has an amazing tail that looks just like a leaf.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffle is unwell. He's in his pyjamas and under strict orders to stay in bed and rest. But poor Waffle can't get comfy. When he sees Evie and Simon playing in the garden, he feels left out and begins to woof and woof. Simon tells Waffle to rest quietly, but it's too late. The wonder dog's incessant barking has already disturbed Mrs Hobbs next door and, to make matters worse, like Waffle she's not feeling well either.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona and Baba help the seagulls defeat the Great Gull.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: It's the day the geese fly over on their annual migration south. When Sonny reports that geese put on an amazing display if you mimic their calls, the gang look for the right sound to get a response from the geese before they fly past.

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs
programme.desc: With only minutes to go before the big performance at McGinty's farm, Maddie has to use all her musical skills to convince Gary the Chicken to perform with the other animals.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels accidentally break the Chickens TV ariel during the season finale of their favourite soap opera. How will they see the finale now.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: The Numberblocks are exploring Fraction Island and meet new Fraction Friends on the way!

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: The Alphablocks have fun naming their hens and, finally, their helper, BEN the pen!

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and his friends learn all about getting their eyes tested. Meanwhile, Mr Tumble helps Grandad Tumble with some new glasses when he loses his old pair.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: The Teletubbies have fun moving backwards and forwards. The Tubby Custard Ride goes forwards, then backwards, then forwards again.

programme.title: Ranger Hamza's Eco Quest
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers discover how grasses play such an important role in the environment. They find out that grasses can be food for animals and some can be turned into ingredients to make food for people too. On the way, they discover how long grass can help shelter small animals and insects, and that cut grass can be useful too, before finding a lovely grassy place to relax and have fun.

programme.title: Football Fantastics
programme.desc: Sophie must infiltrate the sports hall without disturbing a relaxation class Val is running to retrieve bibs, goals and a big bag of footballs.

programme.title: Grace's Amazing Machines
programme.desc: Motorbike rider Grace shows off three amazing machines that all move because of propellers. Grace flies a seaplane, shows off unusual hydrocopter and goes for a fast ride in a raceboat.

programme.title: Dodge's Pup School
programme.desc: An unexpected visitor has arrived in the playground, a cat. The pups are initially scared, after all they've heard that cats have lasers for eyes. But Dodge decides to be brave and go and talk to it.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster is finding it hard to think of something new to do on Do Something New Day, so he heads to Tiniest Fluffiest Bunny's house to see if she has any ideas.

programme.title: Octonauts: Above & Beyond
programme.sub-title: The Octonauts and the Golden Mole
programme.desc: Tracker joins Kwazii in the desert on a mission to prove that he is ready to be an Octo-Agent. During the test, the pair have an encounter with a golden mole just before a storm hits.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Tunnel Rumbler
programme.desc: When Peter and his friends mistake the echoey 'rebbits' of a lost Jeremy Fisher for a scary monster, they set about to rid their tunnels of this mysterious invader. But with Tommy Brock on the warpath nearby, it won't be easy!

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are trying to count how many badges Duggee has when they come across his Counting Badge. He got it for counting to 100. This sounds like hard work.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Zigzag's ADHD gives her a case of the hops, so Vida teaches her how to make a to-do list.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Evil Pea sends a load of Mini Peas to over-run and take over Tato Tower. There's only one person who can help him - the expert pea-yodeller, Prickly Pear.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: The Ducklings throw a 'no adults' party, so Weasy parties solo but makes a huge mess.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Mojo and Bo are great at picking apples with Mojo's Crane Top. But Preston's drone might be even better until it accidentally scoops up Felix. Mojo and Bo are on the chase.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Bluey is unable to get to sleep, so Mum reads her one last story. Unicorse, the most annoying hand puppet in the world, interrupts them, and Bluey tries to reform it.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: Exhausted with requests for help, Looph deliberately disappears. The Piris turn detectives, searching the berg with little success until her ingenious plan is finally revealed.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Mum and Dad have a disagreement, Bluey makes Dad write a love letter to Mum to make up. Now Bluey must find a way to deliver the letter to Mum.

programme.title: Balamory
programme.desc: PC Plum jumps to conclusions when he hears about an upcoming volcanic eruption. But little does he know, it's Ava's science project that's set to fizzle in a safe experiment.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Essam family visit the Brooklyn-Bells. Jess hopes their well-behaved dog, Baxter, will teach Waffle how to be good, but Waffle has other ideas.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: A wayward camping trip teaches Rafi that changing the plan can lead to adventure.

programme.title: JoJo & Gran Gran
programme.desc: Gran Gran's keys get stuck behind the sofa. Inspired by inventors from the past, JoJo and Gran Gran invent a `key collector' to get them back.

programme.title: Sarah & Duck
programme.desc: An excited Sarah and Duck take the Shallots to see the Ribbon Sisters perform with their family circus.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Major Clanger doesn't hear the other Clangers calling him for soup. Tiny and Small fetch him but he decides he must invent a new system so that this won't happen again.

programme.title: BeddyByes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa discover a dandelion clock and have fun blowing the seeds. The Yummies help them plant the seeds, and they marvel at how a seed can become a beautiful flower.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.desc: The Tombliboos are playing their music so fast that everybody does everything very fast too. Igglepiggle and Upsy Daisy dance, and Makka Pakka washes stones until at last, the Tombliboos slow down.

programme.title: Cbeebies Bedtime Stories
programme.sub-title: P-T: Jade Thirlwall: The Dress in the Window
programme.desc: Jade Thirlwall reads a beautiful story about a little boy who dreams of a special outfit. The Dress in the Window is by Robert Tregoning and illustrated by Pippa Curnick.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel.

programme.title: This Is CBeebies
programme.desc: Welcome to the CBeebies channel.

programme.title: Colourblocks
programme.desc: Blue and Yellow meet a new friend: Green. Discover what happens when blue and yellow are mixed.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Baba helps rescue a baby seagull caught up in fishing twine on the beach, proving that he is now as capable as his older sister and friends.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Sula, Pando and Bing discover a bird's nest while making a playhouse in the shed. They try to find the bird by being super quiet, and she flies into their playhouse. When Bing goes in to look for her, she flies out again; they can't both be in the playhouse at the same time. So Bing decides to give the shed to her until her chicks have all grown up.

programme.title: Love Monster
programme.desc: Love Monster's pineapple costume makes it difficult for him to participate in any of the party events.

programme.title: Piggy Builders
programme.desc: Fiona asks the Piggies to help transport her pumpkin to the Vegetable Festival. They agree, not knowing that it is so big, they will have to turn it into a carriage!

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the baby Pea-Rexes are alarmed to discover the source of an awful smell is a flower growing at home-base, even more so when the stench transfers onto Wasabi, who hates baths.

programme.title: Roots and Fruits
programme.desc: Live from the Roots and Fruits theatre, it's Celery performing We're Here to Grow, if he manages to squeeze himself out of the dressing room on time.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston needs Mojo and Bo to pull his big balloon to a place where he can snap a picture of a rare bird. But his balloon gets loose, and Mojo and Bo need to get Preston back on the ground.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Preston needs Mojo and Bo to pull his big balloon to a place where he can snap a picture of a rare bird. But his balloon gets loose, and Mojo and Bo need to get Preston back on the ground.

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.desc: Wilson gets into trouble when he goes to care for an adorable monkey and things start to go wrong.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Carrot is determined to save Supertato from Evil Pea's trap without help from Prickly Pear.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing helps Flop do the shopping at Padget's shop but whilst Flop is paying, he finds a strawberry lolly that he can't resist putting in his pocket. When Flop sees Bing licking the lolly on the way home, they have to decide what to do.

programme.title: Bing
programme.desc: Bing has broken his arm and is struggling to do his usual activities. Petting Arlo hurts, playing with blocks hurts, and even drinking juice hurts. But when Bing and Sula draw together it doesn't hurt.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Norrie is running late to Squirrel Club, so Duggee teaches the squirrels what a commute is: a journey that can take people from home to learn, work or play.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels are confused when they see Duggee sitting on a ball because they like to use it differently. Duggee is quick to explain to the Squirrels that there is no right or wrong way to play and that everyone is different.
